research_title: POS & Scheduling Platform Integration Research for Salon/Beauty Industry
purpose: Identify optimal integration targets for SKINgenius to pull transaction, inventory, appointment, and client data from salon POS and booking systems.

executive_summary.key_finding: The salon industry is fragmented between all-in-one platforms (Vagaro, Fresha) and modular setups (Square POS + Square Appointments, Clover + third-party booking). For SKINgenius, prioritizing platforms with robust REST APIs, webhook support, and high salon market penetration is critical. Square and Vagaro emerge as the top priorities due to market share, API maturity, and data richness.

pos_systems.title: Top 5 POS Systems in Salon/Beauty Industry

systems.name: Square

data_access.transactions: Full access via Payments API, Orders API
data_access.inventory: Catalog API - items, variations, stock levels
data_access.client_records: Customers API - profiles, preferences, purchase history

pricing.api_access: Free (no additional charge beyond transaction fees)

systems.integration_difficulty_notes: Excellent documentation, sandbox environment, OAuth 2.0 flow. Webhooks well-supported. Most developer-friendly.
gotchas: OAuth tokens expire and require refresh token rotation
Webhook signatures must be validated for security
Inventory webhooks have slight delay (not truly real-time)
Customer notes/fields are limited to basic types

systems.name: Clover

data_access.transactions: Ecommerce API + Orders API - payment records
data_access.inventory: Inventory API - items, stock levels, categories
data_access.client_records: Customers API - limited compared to Square

rate_limits.default: Not publicly documented - believed to be 10 req/sec

systems.integration_difficulty_notes: Solid REST API but requires Android app for deep POS integration. Approval process for developer program. Good for building apps ON the Clover platform.
gotchas: Developer program approval required
API access tied to merchant app installation
Less salon-specific features out of the box
Webhook documentation is sparse
Customer data model is less flexible than Square
salon_specific_features: Can integrate with third-party booking via apps
Service menu support via custom items

systems.name: Vagaro POS

data_access.client_records: Customers API - contact info, tags, service history

rate_limits.default: Not publicly specified - implement exponential backoff
rate_limits.notes: Contact Vagaro for enterprise rate limits

systems.integration_difficulty_notes: All-in-one platform means POS data is deeply integrated with scheduling. API is newer (launched ~2023) but growing. Webhook support for real-time updates.
gotchas: API is relatively new - may have stability issues
OAuth not available - API key based auth
Rate limits not clearly documented
Webhook event types are limited compared to Square
Some advanced POS features may not be API-accessible
salon_specific_features: Built-in for salons - service menus, packages, memberships
Inventory tied to retail and professional products

systems.name: Lightspeed Retail

rate_limits.burst: May allow short bursts above limit

systems.integration_difficulty_notes: More retail-focused than salon-focused. API is mature but less tailored to service-based businesses. Good for salons with heavy retail inventory.
gotchas: More retail-oriented - service booking is weaker
Lower rate limits (60/min)
OAuth 1.0a (older standard)
Less salon-specific webhook events
Customer relationship features are limited
salon_specific_features: Service items can be configured as products

systems.name: Shopify POS

data_access.product_sales: Order line items with product references

systems.integration_difficulty_notes: Excellent API ecosystem but retail-focused. Shopify POS Pro adds staff and location features. Good for salons with significant retail operations.
gotchas: POS-specific features require Shopify POS Pro ($89/mo)
Appointment booking not native - requires third-party app
Rate limits are strict for non-Plus merchants
GraphQL API is newer and recommended for complex queries
Staff/employee API is limited compared to dedicated salon systems

scheduling_platforms.title: Top 5 Scheduling/Booking Platforms in Salon Industry

platforms.name: Vagaro

data_access.appointments: Full CRUD - status, time, service, provider, client
data_access.service_types: Services API - name, duration, price, category

platforms.integration_difficulty_notes: All-in-one means scheduling + POS data in one place. API is newer but comprehensive for beauty/wellness. Webhooks available for real-time sync.
gotchas: API key authentication (no OAuth)
Rate limits unclear - implement backoff
Webhook event types are limited (appointments, customers, transactions, employees, form responses, locations)
Some advanced features may not be API-accessible
Multi-location requires specific business ID handling
salon_specific_features: Built for salons - service categories, add-ons, packages

platforms.name: Square Appointments

data_access.service_types: Catalog API - service items with duration

platforms.integration_difficulty_notes: Seamlessly integrates with Square POS. Same OAuth and webhook infrastructure. Best developer experience for appointment + transaction data together.
gotchas: Requires Square Appointments subscription (free tier available)
Multi-location requires proper location scoping
Service variations can be complex in catalog structure
Booking webhooks don't include customer details - need separate fetch
Staff availability rules are not fully API-exposable
salon_specific_features: Service duration and buffer time support

platforms.name: Fresha
platforms.integration_difficulty_notes: Modern API with good documentation. Free tier is compelling. Strong internationally. Good webhook support. Partner program required for API access.
gotchas: Partner program application required
Free salon tier means they may not have premium features
API is partner-focused, not merchant self-serve
Some advanced scheduling rules not exposed
International focus - US market share growing but smaller

platforms.name: Boulevard

data_access.appointments: Appointments query - detailed with client, service, provider
data_access.service_types: Services query with pricing and duration

platforms.integration_difficulty_notes: Premium platform with modern GraphQL API. Targets high-end salons and medspas. Smaller market share but high-value clients. Good for premium salon integration.
gotchas: Premium pricing means smaller addressable market
GraphQL learning curve if team is unfamiliar
Smaller user base = fewer salons to integrate
Webhook documentation is limited
API access may require enterprise agreement

platforms.name: Acuity Scheduling

data_access.appointments: Appointments endpoint - time, client, service, notes

platforms.integration_difficulty_notes: Simple, clean API. Good for basic appointment data. Not salon-specific but widely used. Easy to integrate for core scheduling data.
gotchas: Not salon-specific - lacks service menu depth
No native POS integration (relies on integrations)
Client data model is simpler than dedicated salon platforms
No inventory/product sales tracking
Webhook events are basic (appointment created/modified/cancelled)

combinations.name: Square All-in-One
combinations.integration_approach: Single OAuth flow, unified API, shared customer/transaction/appointment data
cons: May outgrow for large multi-location chains

combinations.name: Vagaro All-in-One
combinations.integration_approach: Single API key, POS + scheduling data in one platform

combinations.name: Clover + Third-Party Booking
combinations.integration_approach: Two separate integrations - POS data from Clover, scheduling from booking platform
cons: Data silos between POS and scheduling

combinations.name: Fresha + External POS
combinations.integration_approach: Fresha handles scheduling, separate POS for transactions

tiers.rationale: Combined 55-65% salon market share. Mature APIs. Rich transaction + appointment + customer data. Webhook support for real-time updates.
tiers.expected_data: Full customer profiles, purchase history, appointment history, service preferences, retail product purchases

tiers.rationale: Fresha is strong internationally and growing. Square Appointments covers salons using Square POS. Both have solid APIs.
tiers.effort: Medium - Fresha requires partner program, Square is straightforward
tiers.expected_data: Appointment history, client data, service types, some transaction data

tiers.rationale: Clover for multi-location chains. Boulevard for premium salons. Smaller market share but valuable segments.
tiers.effort: Higher - Clover requires dev program approval, Boulevard is GraphQL

tiers.rationale: Acuity is simpler but less salon-specific. Mindbody is fitness-focused. Booksy is growing but API is limited.

recommendations.advice: Implement OAuth 2.0 for Square, Clover, Fresha, Boulevard. API keys for Vagaro. Store tokens securely with refresh logic.
Use webhooks for real-time updates (appointment created, transaction completed). Implement signature validation, idempotency, and exponential backoff for retries.
Full sync on initial connection, then incremental via webhooks + periodic polling as fallback. Store sync cursor/timestamp per integration.
Implement client-side rate limiters per platform. Queue requests when approaching limits. Use batch operations where available (Square).
Expect transient failures. Log all API errors with context. Alert on sustained failures. Implement circuit breaker for degraded platforms.
Challenge: Customer in POS may not match customer in booking system. Build matching logic (phone, email, name). Create unified customer profile in SKINgenius.
Each platform has different service/product models. Create normalized schema: Customer, Appointment, Service, Product, Transaction, Employee. Map platform-specific fields to normalized model.

entities.use_for_skingenius: Personalized skincare recommendations, ingredient preference learning, routine timing based on visit patterns
Correlate treatments with skin condition changes, recommend pre/post-treatment skincare
Understand treatment types, recommend compatible at-home products
Ingredient analysis, retail recommendations, trending products
Purchase pattern analysis, product affinity, spending behavior

items.description: Salon customer data is PII. Ensure GDPR/CCPA compliance. Get explicit consent for data sharing. Implement data retention policies.
Tokens expire. Implement refresh token logic. Handle re-auth gracefully. Don't lose data on token expiry.
Webhooks can be missed. Implement idempotency keys. Have polling fallback for critical data.
Limits change, some undocumented. Monitor 429 responses. Implement adaptive rate limiting.
Customer may visit multiple locations. Location scoping varies by API. Plan for cross-location identity resolution.
Test thoroughly in production. Sandbox may not have all features (e.g., webhooks sometimes behave differently).

next_steps.title: Recommended Next Steps for SKINgenius
actions: 1. Create developer accounts on Square, Vagaro, and Fresha
2. Build proof-of-concept integration with Square (highest market share + best dev experience)
3. Implement OAuth flow and webhook handlers
4. Build normalized data model mapping Square objects to SKINgenius schema
5. Test with a pilot salon partner
7. Design unified customer identity resolution logic
8. Plan for data privacy compliance (consent flows, data retention)

sources: Industry knowledge and Capterra/G2 platform reviews

metadata.author: SKINgenius Research Agent
metadata.confidence_level: High for market share estimates (industry consensus), Medium for specific rate limits (may vary)
metadata.review_cycle: Quarterly or when major platform API changes announced
